I am from West Bengal. The reality on the ground in West Bengal is far different from the media narrative. In many constituencies, voters don’t even know the names of BJP candidates, strong local faces are simply missing.

This makes TMC the clear favourite to win most seats, an easy “Yes” bet on Polymarket. Online BJP supporters live in their own bubble. They believe these things:

1. All deleted voters were TMC voters.

2. All 58 lakh fake votes (dead people, displaced people, etc.) in 2024 went to TMC.

3. Every Hindu will vote for BJP.

4. All Muslims will vote for Humayun Kabir or ISF.

Bookmark this.
When the bubble bursts on 4th May, come back and read it again.

The significantly higher Phase One voter turnout percentage compared to any previous West Bengal election also suggests that a lot of the names removed during the SIR weren't voters in previous elections. The SIR will have less of an impact than people think

I’m from Kolkata, and here’s what you need to know:

15 years of TMC rule has pushed anti-incumbency to the peak. People are done with corruption, cut money, and syndicate raj.

BJP has come in with a clear plan — not just talk. Their welfare push directly challenges TMC’s base, especially women voters.

The recruitment scam broke trust completely. Hardworking candidates got sidelined while jobs were sold — people haven’t forgotten.

RG Kar incident exposed serious law and order failure. Women’s safety is now a central issue and BJP is taking it head-on.

Hindu consolidation is visibly stronger this time. Matua voters and refugee communities are firmly backing BJP over citizenship guarantees.

The voter list cleanup has changed the electoral math on the ground. It’s a big structural shift.

“Double engine” is a real advantage. People want alignment with the Centre for faster development and investment.

Youth anger is massive. No jobs, no recruitment — BJP is the only party directly addressing it.

BJP already built a strong base in 2021. This time it’s about converting that support into seats and groundwork reflects that.

TMC still has its network, but the momentum on the ground is clearly shifting.

This is the strongest position BJP has ever been in Bengal.

ground reality in Bengal is clear: this election is a straight freebies war.

TMC side: Lakshmir Bhandar ₹500–₹1000/month already running at scale, Kanyashree with annual support + ₹25K at 18, Rupashree ₹25K marriage grant, Swasthya Sathi free health coverage, plus years of built-out delivery network that actually reaches people.

BJP side: going aggressive to outbid Lakshmir Bhandar expansion up to ~₹3000/month, ₹25K Kanyashree-style payout, ₹25K Rupashree, 7th Pay Commission, 33% reservation for women in govt jobs, expanded free healthcare (diagnostics/cancer), SHG loan support, and the “double engine” development pitch.

This isn’t ideology vs ideology it’s existing welfare vs bigger promised welfare. Voters are comparing who gives more and who they trust to deliver.
